page: Microsoft Azure DC48as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor runs at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and Geekbench multi-core scores all placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a strong PassMark CPU encryption test result, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instructions. Memory performance is also robust, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, though uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier at 87.89 GB/sec and 46.87 GB/sec respectively.

This server is well-suited for multi-threaded computational workloads, image processing, and ray tracing, which both register strong performance in Geekbench tests. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in small-scale LLM inference, processing 2601.51 tokens per second on a 135M model. However, for larger models such as a 70B parameter LLM, text generation speed drops to a poor 2.53 tokens per second, indicating a clear limitation for heavy AI generation tasks without dedicated GPU acceleration. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which should be factored into database and I/O-heavy deployments.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC48as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture and Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng and PassMark CPU benchmarks. It features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. While it excels in multi-threaded tasks, cryptography, and small-scale LLM inference, its text generation performance on large 70B LLM models is limited. This server is best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, multi-threaded data processing, image rendering, and web serving workloads that utilize external Premium Storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC48as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and strong multi-core performance, suitable for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC48as_cc_v5 is a 48-vCPU general-purpose instance with 192 GB RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced 48-vCPU virtual machine delivering strong multi-core processing and secure cryptography for diverse enterprise and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 192 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Delivers strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on external Premium Storage
Best suited for multi-threaded data processing, image rendering, and web serving
